title: [FIRRTL] need to lower procedural high firrtl (e.g. whens) to single assignment low-firrtl semantics
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module mod_0 :
    input clock: Clock
    input reset: UInt<1>
    input d: UInt<1>
    output q: UInt<1>
    q <= d
  module top_mod :
    input clock: Clock
    input reset: UInt<1>
    input d: UInt<1>
    output q: UInt<1>
    inst U0 of mod_0
    U0 is invalid
    U0.clock <= clock
    U0.reset <= reset
    U0.d <= d
    q <= U0.q
```

Compiled with `firtool --lower-to-rtl --enable-lower-types` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
// Standard header to adapt well known macros to our needs.
...
module mod_0(
  input  clock, reset, d,
  output q);

  assign q = d;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3
endmodule

module top_mod(
  input  clock, reset, d,
  output q);

  wire _T;	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5
  wire _T_0;	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5
  wire _T_1;	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5
  wire U0_q;	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5

  assign _T = clock;	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5
  assign _T = 1'h0;	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5, :14:5
  assign _T_0 = reset;	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5
  assign _T_0 = 1'h0;	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5, :14:5
  assign _T_1 = d;	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5
  assign _T_1 = 1'h0;	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5, :14:5
  mod_0 U0 (	// a_top_mod.fir:13:5
    .clock (_T),
    .reset (_T_0),
    .d     (_T_1),
    .q     (U0_q)
  );
  assign q = U0_q;	// a_top_mod.fir:8:3
endmodule
```

Which has incorrect reassignment or `_T`, `_T_0` and `_T_1` temporal wires.

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5.0` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module mod_0(
  input   d,
  output  q
);
  assign q = d;
endmodule
module top_mod(
  input   clock,
  input   reset,
  input   d,
  output  q
);
  wire  U0_d;
  wire  U0_q;
  mod_0 U0 (
    .d(U0_d),
    .q(U0_q)
  );
  assign q = U0_q;
  assign U0_d = d;
endmodule
```
